title: lonicera douglasii var. dasygyna
intro: Lonicera douglasii var. dasygyna, also known as Douglas' honeysuckle or red honeysuckle, is a deciduous shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Lonicera douglasii var. dasygyna include relief from bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate headaches. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and stems are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
